steps.title: Identify the main verb and the constraint on the blank
steps.content: The sentence's main clause is “Such meticulous documentation of mundane information... became Kawara's life's work,” where “became” is the only main verb. The blank must add description of what the documentation accomplishes without introducing a second main verb.

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
A comma before a participle introduces a participial phrase—a verb form that modifies a noun without creating a second main clause.

Choice C uses this correctly: the comma before “generating artworks” signals it describes the documentation rather than competing with “became.” A and B introduce actual competing main verbs (“has generated” and “generates”), while D's “generated” is ambiguous—it could be a second main verb or a past participle.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
